title: Silent excerpt corruption discovered only after years of trust
scenario_description: A passage is excerpted or normalized wrongly, looks fine in the library, is studied and trusted for years, and is only later caught by opening the original book.
failure_chain: source text -> excerpting/normalization distortion -> trusted study -> delayed comparison with original -> trust collapse
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: It proves that the library can feed false knowledge while appearing safe, which makes the rest of the corpus suspect.
direct_damage_to_study: Wrong rulings, wrong memorization, wrong explanations repeated with confidence.
long_horizon_damage: Years of learning become suspect, and later teaching or practice may already have been affected.

title: A loud grave mistake reveals the library is unsafe
scenario_description: A major obvious mistake appears in the library during normal study, showing that the validation regime let something severe through.
failure_chain: pipeline defect -> obvious wrong output -> user notices during study -> confidence in validation collapses
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: The issue is visible, but what destroys trust is that a severe error was able to survive long enough to reach study use.
direct_damage_to_study: Study pauses immediately and current session becomes unreliable.
long_horizon_damage: Validation discipline itself comes under suspicion.

title: One local knowledge error poisons global trust
scenario_description: One confirmed content error is found, but the system cannot convincingly show that the rest of the learned knowledge is unaffected.
failure_chain: local error discovered -> blast radius unclear -> user cannot quarantine affected knowledge -> all library-derived knowledge becomes suspect
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: The danger is not the single error by itself but the inability to keep that error local.
direct_damage_to_study: Everything previously learned through the library becomes questionable.
long_horizon_damage: Permanent suspicion of all future study unless a full audit is possible.

title: Wrong cutting, merging, or granulation changes meaning
scenario_description: The system cuts an excerpt too early, merges unrelated material, or atomizes a unit so badly that the meaning or study function changes.
failure_chain: bad boundary decision -> distorted study unit -> wrong understanding or unreadable atomization -> false learning
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: It attacks the core promise that the library gives safe, usable study units instead of corrupted fragments.
direct_damage_to_study: The user studies malformed units and memorizes distorted relations or incomplete claims.
long_horizon_damage: Systematic branch-level misunderstanding can accumulate over years.

title: Architecture built on loose grounds forces cascading rework
scenario_description: A core architectural assumption or engine contract turns out to be weak after multiple engines have already been built around it.
failure_chain: weak early design -> downstream dependencies accrete -> better design discovered late -> cascading breakage or permanent compromise
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: Even if current outputs still function, the whole system becomes historically contaminated by avoidable weakness.
direct_damage_to_study: Study may continue on top of a system known to be less than optimal or less than secure.
long_horizon_damage: Years of work can be spent deepening the wrong architecture.

title: Progress is lost
scenario_description: Development products, curated outputs, or study artifacts are lost because progress was not preserved safely enough.
failure_chain: weak persistence/backup -> file or state loss -> missing outputs -> reruns or unrecoverable gaps
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: A lifetime library cannot rely on fragile memory.
direct_damage_to_study: Study continuity breaks; prior effort may need repeating.
long_horizon_damage: Confidence in the system as a life-project store declines sharply.

title: The app or usable library shell is lost
scenario_description: The surrounding application or access layer is lost, leaving the data inaccessible or practically unusable.
failure_chain: app loss / access-layer failure -> inability to navigate or use knowledge -> project paralysis
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: A library that cannot be accessed as a library has failed its mission even if raw files survive somewhere.
direct_damage_to_study: Normal study becomes impossible.
long_horizon_damage: Forced migration or partial loss may permanently damage continuity.

title: Years later the owner realizes the design capped scholarship
scenario_description: The library functions, but years later it becomes clear that a better architecture, research path, tool choice, or representation would have produced much stronger scholarship.
failure_chain: suboptimal design accepted -> years of study on weaker system -> later comparison/regret -> confidence in the life-project weakens
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: The project is judged not only by correctness but by whether it gave the highest possible environment for learning.
direct_damage_to_study: The owner studies less efficiently and with less reach than was possible.
long_horizon_damage: Irrecoverable opportunity loss measured in years of life and scholarship.

title: Cost blow-up makes full-scale build impossible
scenario_description: Per-book or per-run costs scale into an impossible budget for the intended total corpus.
failure_chain: expensive pipeline choices -> budget cap -> incomplete corpus -> capped scholarship
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: A library that cannot afford to ingest the intended body of knowledge cannot fulfill the mission.
direct_damage_to_study: Coverage remains partial and strategically constrained by money.
long_horizon_damage: Blind spots harden into the owner’s knowledge because inaccessible sources never enter the system.

title: Latency and throughput ceilings make corpus-scale use unrealistic
scenario_description: Processing is so slow that the dream of digesting the full body of relevant knowledge becomes operationally impossible.
failure_chain: slow pipeline -> days per book or similar -> backlog explodes -> corpus never completes
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: The project becomes theoretically impressive but practically non-viable.
direct_damage_to_study: The owner studies from a perpetually incomplete or stale library.
long_horizon_damage: Scale failure becomes a hidden ceiling on scholarship.

title: Extreme granulation atomizes study units into unreadable fragments
scenario_description: Excerpting becomes so fine-grained that units are effectively sentences or atoms rather than coherent study material.
failure_chain: over-granulation -> unreadable fragments -> user must reconstruct manually -> library loses its purpose
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: The library stops easing study and starts creating new chaos.
direct_damage_to_study: Reading and memorization become exhausting and fragmented.
long_horizon_damage: The owner wastes time reconstructing what the library should already have made coherent.

title: Collected data is saved badly and poisons later local-model training
scenario_description: The structured outputs being collected now are saved in a form that later makes local training, reuse, or trustworthy derivation unsafe or ineffective.
failure_chain: bad dataset structure -> weak or contaminated training corpus -> local model inherits flaws -> future outputs multiply the damage
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: It converts today's preservation mistakes into tomorrow's multiplied intelligence errors.
direct_damage_to_study: Future assistant/model behavior becomes less reliable or misleading.
long_horizon_damage: Bad data compounds instead of merely sitting inert.

title: Best available tools, models, APIs, or libraries are not used when they materially matter
scenario_description: The build proceeds without identifying or integrating tools that could unlock better quality, feasibility, or coverage.
failure_chain: weak tech survey -> weaker capabilities -> avoidable constraints -> long-term regret
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: The owner asked for an unprecedented library, not a merely competent one.
direct_damage_to_study: The library underperforms its possible ceiling.
long_horizon_damage: Years may pass before the missed capability is obvious and by then rework may be costly.

title: Agents build the project inside a suboptimal environment
scenario_description: The agents lack the best skills, hooks, MCPs, tools, configs, workflows, or autonomy to demand improvements to their own environment.
failure_chain: constrained agent environment -> weaker execution/research/testing -> hidden defects or missed opportunities -> degraded library
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: A constrained builder can quietly become the ceiling of the whole project.
direct_damage_to_study: The finished library may be less safe or less capable than it could have been.
long_horizon_damage: Suboptimal build conditions create systemic mediocrity rather than visible single bugs.

title: Not enough research is done before building high-risk parts
scenario_description: Important assumptions are not deeply researched even though powerful research models are available.
failure_chain: insufficient research -> loose assumption accepted -> build proceeds -> hidden defect emerges later
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: The project explicitly depends on deep reasoning; skipping research is not a small process flaw but a foundational one.
direct_damage_to_study: The owner studies from outputs built on inadequately examined assumptions.
long_horizon_damage: Late-discovered design weakness can invalidate large downstream investments.

title: Agent blind spots and unclear roles leave serious issues undiscovered
scenario_description: Collaboration between agents remains informal, role boundaries are weak, and important classes of error are simply never noticed.
failure_chain: unclear collaboration -> blind spots survive -> false confidence in coverage -> defects enter system
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: High-stakes systems cannot rely on hope that someone would have noticed.
direct_damage_to_study: Unsafe outputs survive into the library because nobody was formally responsible for catching them.
long_horizon_damage: Repeated oversight becomes normalized rather than exceptional.

title: Creativity failure: the library becomes good but not unprecedented
scenario_description: The system works, but it never identifies genuinely better possibilities and therefore never reaches the intended extraordinary study environment.
failure_chain: conventional design mindset -> missed possibilities -> merely good system -> capped scholarly edge
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: For this project, failure to find the best possible path is itself a substantive failure.
direct_damage_to_study: The owner studies in a library that is less powerful than it should have been.
long_horizon_damage: Lost scholarly edge compounds across years.

title: Source provenance is too weak to audit a suspected error
scenario_description: A suspicious piece of knowledge appears in the library, but the system cannot clearly show the exact source, edition, page, processing lineage, or transformation history that produced it.
failure_chain: weak lineage -> suspected problem -> no clean audit path -> local suspicion becomes broader mistrust
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: Even if the content later proves right, the inability to audit it is itself a violation of trustworthiness.
direct_damage_to_study: Study pauses because the owner cannot verify what he is looking at.
long_horizon_damage: Every later question about correctness becomes more expensive and less decisive.

title: Wrong source, edition, compiler, or editor identity enters the pipeline
scenario_description: The pipeline confuses which work or edition is being handled, or collapses compiler, editor, and author roles incorrectly.
failure_chain: source identity confusion -> wrong metadata and lineage -> downstream attribution and content interpretation errors
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: Even accurate-looking excerpts become unsafe when attached to the wrong scholarly object.
direct_damage_to_study: The owner studies and cites knowledge under the wrong source frame.
long_horizon_damage: School, author, and proof relations become unreliable across the corpus.

title: Attribution error assigns a view to the wrong scholar or school
scenario_description: A view, date, school, or author-layer is attributed incorrectly, causing the user to memorize false scholarly ownership.
failure_chain: attribution mistake -> false ownership -> wrong comparative understanding -> wrong teaching
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: The library would be rewriting the scholarly map rather than representing it.
direct_damage_to_study: The owner learns and later transmits false scholarly positions.
long_horizon_damage: Comparative fiqh, proof analysis, and inherited study all become corrupted.

title: Quote-layer mistake turns borrowed proof into direct authorial stance
scenario_description: A quoted scholar's proof or discussion is flattened so that it appears to be the current author's direct statement or vice versa.
failure_chain: layer confusion -> wrong functional reading -> wrong attribution of proof or rule -> false understanding
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: It produces scholarly-looking output that is structurally false in a way many users would not catch.
direct_damage_to_study: The owner memorizes the wrong argumentative structure and may misteach who held what and why.
long_horizon_damage: Trust in quoted material across the library weakens.

title: Taxonomy misplacement teaches the right text under the wrong branch
scenario_description: Content is placed under the wrong science, topic, or leaf, so the user studies it in the wrong conceptual environment.
failure_chain: misplacement -> wrong local neighbors and boundaries -> wrong mental map -> wrong downstream reasoning
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: The tree is not decoration; it determines how knowledge is understood and remembered.
direct_damage_to_study: The owner copies a wrong conceptual map into his mind.
long_horizon_damage: Misplaced knowledge becomes harder to unlearn because it is memorized with the wrong neighbors.

title: Rule, proof, example, or relation links are broken or incomplete
scenario_description: The library stores pieces of knowledge but loses the links that show what proves what, what explains what, or which examples belong to which point.
failure_chain: relation loss -> user sees isolated pieces -> wrong integration by memory -> degraded mastery
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: A library built for structured study fails if it silently drops the very structure it claims to provide.
direct_damage_to_study: More effort goes into reconstruction and guesswork than into memorization.
long_horizon_damage: The owner may mistake missing relation integrity for his own weak understanding.

title: Cuts are made without omission honesty
scenario_description: Text is removed during excerpting, but the resulting unit looks as though it is uninterrupted source flow with nothing missing.
failure_chain: silent cut -> deceptive cleanliness -> user reads with false confidence -> hidden context loss or topic shift
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: The user is being deceived about what the source actually looked like.
direct_damage_to_study: The owner may memorize a cleaned artifact rather than a faithful study unit.
long_horizon_damage: Later audit becomes harder because the user cannot tell what was original and what was excerpting surgery.

title: Context loss creates false certainty rather than obvious confusion
scenario_description: A supposedly self-contained excerpt is understandable enough to be memorized, but missing context makes its meaning narrower, broader, or differently situated than the user realizes.
failure_chain: partial self-containment -> plausible reading -> confident memorization -> later contextual mismatch
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: This is worse than obvious confusion because the user does not know he is missing something.
direct_damage_to_study: Wrongly shaped knowledge gets fixed in memory.
long_horizon_damage: The owner may teach a context-poor version as though it were full knowledge.

title: Search or ranking surfaces plausible but not best evidence
scenario_description: Retrieval and ranking technically work, but they elevate less relevant or less authoritative material in ways the owner may not notice.
failure_chain: ranking bias -> skewed reading order -> distorted sense of centrality or consensus -> long-term study bias
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: The library can quietly steer study without corrupting any individual passage.
direct_damage_to_study: The owner spends time on the wrong pieces first and forms skewed emphasis.
long_horizon_damage: A biased reading path can produce a biased scholarly shape in the mind.

title: The interface creates false confidence by hiding uncertainty and lineage
scenario_description: The library presents knowledge as polished and settled while hiding confidence, unresolved issues, weak provenance, or validation gaps.
failure_chain: uncertainty hidden -> user overtrusts -> faster memorization of risky output -> later shock when issue surfaces
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: The danger is not only the underlying defect but the interface encouraging overtrust.
direct_damage_to_study: The owner studies risky content as if it were secure.
long_horizon_damage: The more polished the illusion, the more devastating the later correction.

title: Mention-versus-topic mistakes pollute the tree or hide real topics
scenario_description: The system either creates branches for mere passing mentions or fails to create branches where a true topic is substantively present.
failure_chain: bad topic threshold -> polluted or starved taxonomy -> misdirected study and missing structure
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: The owner relies on the tree to reflect the real shape of knowledge, not noise or omissions.
direct_damage_to_study: The user either wastes time on pseudo-topics or misses real ones.
long_horizon_damage: Mental maps become noisier or poorer than the actual science.

title: Footnotes are handled in a way that swallows or loses meaningful knowledge
scenario_description: Footnotes are either always glued, always split, or otherwise mishandled so that important content is lost, drowned, or wrongly isolated.
failure_chain: crude footnote policy -> relevant note lost or irrelevant mass retained -> study friction or hidden missing knowledge
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: Footnotes can contain real scholarly substance; mishandling them changes what the library actually represents.
direct_damage_to_study: The owner either misses relevant notes or is dragged through irrelevant editorial mass.
long_horizon_damage: Whole classes of knowledge become inconsistently represented.

title: OCR or scan handling silently changes Arabic meaning
scenario_description: OCR, scan cleanup, encoding, or normalization alters Arabic text subtly enough that the change is not noticed during study.
failure_chain: scan/OCR error -> altered Arabic -> excerpt inherits altered wording -> false knowledge enters memory
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: It means the library can rewrite source wording before the owner ever sees it.
direct_damage_to_study: The owner memorizes and teaches statements that never existed in the source.
long_horizon_damage: Trust in all scanned or OCRed content collapses.

title: Source version or edition drift changes what the library means over time
scenario_description: The same work is processed from different editions or changed source files without clear version control, so knowledge shifts silently across time.
failure_chain: edition/version drift -> same-looking item changes underneath -> notes/training/study mismatch -> trust erosion
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: The owner can no longer know whether a remembered excerpt still refers to the same underlying source state.
direct_damage_to_study: Repetition and recall collide with moving targets.
long_horizon_damage: Past notes, training data, and citations become unstable.

title: Metadata poisoning propagates one wrong label through many downstream artifacts
scenario_description: One wrong metadata field such as school, science, or author enters early and is reused everywhere downstream.
failure_chain: bad metadata -> downstream reuse -> many wrong placements/filters/views -> broad corruption from one seed
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: A single early metadata defect can distort a whole family of outputs while every later component appears to function normally.
direct_damage_to_study: The owner studies a systematically skewed library without noticing the common root cause.
long_horizon_damage: Corrections later require wide reprocessing and may not fully fix learned damage.

title: Deduplication and contradiction handling fail, leaving multiple inconsistent versions of the same knowledge
scenario_description: The same content enters through multiple sources or editions and receives inconsistent metadata or treatment without clear contradiction handling.
failure_chain: duplicate entry -> divergent processing -> inconsistent outputs -> user cannot tell which is canonical
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: The library starts to behave like conflicting notes rather than a controlled system.
direct_damage_to_study: The owner wastes study effort adjudicating the library instead of using it.
long_horizon_damage: Repeated contradiction trains suspicion even where the library is right.

title: Cheap fallback behavior silently lowers quality in exactly the cases that matter most
scenario_description: Cost or speed pressure causes the system to rely on weaker models or lower-quality paths for difficult cases without making the downgrade obvious.
failure_chain: optimization pressure -> hidden weaker path -> edge cases handled poorly -> high-stakes errors concentrate in hard cases
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: It creates a system that looks efficient while quietly sacrificing the cases where rigor is most needed.
direct_damage_to_study: Rare, subtle, or ambiguous material becomes the least trustworthy while still appearing equally polished.
long_horizon_damage: The user may never notice the pattern until many edge-case failures have accumulated.

title: Evaluation is rigorous-looking but hollow, so unsafe behavior passes validation
scenario_description: Tests, examples, or benchmarks look thorough but mainly exercise happy paths and fail to expose the real edge cases.
failure_chain: hollow evaluation -> false confidence -> unsafe system shipped -> real-world failure later
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: It simulates rigor without actually providing safety.
direct_damage_to_study: The owner trusts outputs because they were 'tested' when the tests did not actually prove safety.
long_horizon_damage: Bad validation habits can bless many later defects.

title: Human-gate theater asks the owner to approve what he cannot truly verify
scenario_description: The system routes domain-critical correctness questions to the owner in ways that create an illusion of safety without real verification.
failure_chain: weak gate design -> owner shown plausible output -> approval mistaken for correctness -> unsafe content enters library
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: It weaponizes the owner's decision authority without respecting the limits of what he can personally audit.
direct_damage_to_study: Unsafe content may enter under a false sense of human oversight.
long_horizon_damage: The approval process becomes part of the illusion rather than the safety mechanism.

title: Dependency disappearance or lock-in traps the lifetime library
scenario_description: A provider, API, storage platform, or dependency disappears or changes terms, and the library cannot be migrated cleanly without loss or major interruption.
failure_chain: external dependency change -> forced migration -> missing exportability or compatibility -> access or continuity damage
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: A lifetime knowledge system must outlive any one vendor or service contract.
direct_damage_to_study: Access breaks, migration work interrupts learning, or data arrives in degraded form.
long_horizon_damage: The project becomes strategically fragile rather than sovereign.

title: Backups exist but are corrupted, incomplete, or unusable under pressure
scenario_description: Recovery systems look present on paper, but when needed they do not restore a trustworthy working state.
failure_chain: weak backup discipline -> disaster event -> restore failure -> project memory loss
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: A false sense of backup safety is worse than openly having none.
direct_damage_to_study: Years of work can disappear or return in an unreliable state.
long_horizon_damage: The owner can no longer treat the library as a durable inheritance project.

title: Pipeline defects contaminate future local-model training data
scenario_description: Corrupted or weakly structured outputs are later used as training data, causing future local models to absorb and reproduce the same failures at scale.
failure_chain: pipeline defect -> saved dataset -> local model training -> learned corruption multiplied through future use
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: It turns one generation of defects into a self-amplifying future system.
direct_damage_to_study: Later assistant outputs become systemically warped, not just occasionally wrong.
long_horizon_damage: The project's future intelligence layer inherits hidden poison from its training substrate.

title: The system works on demos but hits a real scale ceiling on the full corpus
scenario_description: The architecture proves itself on a small set of books but breaks economically, operationally, or qualitatively when pushed to the real intended scale.
failure_chain: small-scale success -> premature confidence -> scale-up -> collapse in cost/speed/quality -> mission failure
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: The owner's mission is explicitly full-scale; a demo-safe system is not enough.
direct_damage_to_study: The library stays partial and therefore misleadingly complete-looking.
long_horizon_damage: Years of build effort may still fail to produce the intended lifetime corpus.

title: A bad schema or storage choice becomes an irreversible long-term burden
scenario_description: Early data-model choices omit fields, lineage, or structure that later turn out to be necessary for export, audit, migration, or training.
failure_chain: weak schema -> data saved without future needs -> later need emerges -> painful migration or loss
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: The project starts eating its future options because of an avoidable structural choice.
direct_damage_to_study: Current study may continue, but future reuse, audit, and scaling get harder.
long_horizon_damage: Institutional memory becomes trapped in the wrong format.

title: The library is mostly right but wrong in the rare edge cases that matter most
scenario_description: Aggregate performance looks strong, but the difficult minority cases — where nuance matters most — are exactly where the system fails.
failure_chain: high average success -> hidden edge-case weakness -> owner overtrusts -> critical mistakes in subtle matters
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: This project is not satisfied by average correctness if the hardest knowledge is where the system collapses.
direct_damage_to_study: The owner becomes strongest where the library is easy and weakest where the scholarly edge is most needed.
long_horizon_damage: The library quietly caps depth while advertising breadth.

title: A bug is fixed later but the affected knowledge cannot be bounded cleanly
scenario_description: The engineering team knows a defect existed and has fixed it, but cannot precisely tell which outputs, study sessions, or trained data were contaminated.
failure_chain: latent defect -> fix shipped -> missing blast-radius traceability -> trust remains poisoned
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: Repair without containment still leaves the owner unable to trust prior knowledge.
direct_damage_to_study: Repetition and review are contaminated by uncertainty about what was learned through the bad period.
long_horizon_damage: Every fixed bug becomes a permanent scar unless impact can be localized.

title: Prestige illusion: the library looks advanced and comprehensive but is actually unsafe
scenario_description: The library feels visually advanced, highly structured, and impressive enough to create confidence disproportionate to its actual verification strength.
failure_chain: polished presentation -> faster overtrust -> deeper dependence before audit -> larger later damage
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: Unsafe systems become more dangerous when they look sophisticated enough to suppress skepticism.
direct_damage_to_study: The owner studies more aggressively and with less caution than the backend deserves.
long_horizon_damage: The eventual trust collapse is deeper because the illusion was stronger.

title: Multi-agent outputs conflict and no accountable arbitration exists
scenario_description: Different agents or workflows produce conflicting outputs, but there is no clear arbitration regime, audit history, or ownership of the final decision.
failure_chain: multi-agent divergence -> unresolved conflict -> arbitrary merge or silent overwrite -> unsafe final output
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: Collaboration without accountable resolution turns plurality into noise rather than safety.
direct_damage_to_study: The owner may study whichever answer happened to win procedurally rather than substantively.
long_horizon_damage: Contradictory institutional memory accumulates.

title: An apparently small flaw compounds for years and becomes catastrophic
scenario_description: A defect that seems minor in one session quietly compounds through repetition, memorization, teaching, schema reuse, or model training until it becomes a major project-level problem.
failure_chain: small flaw ignored -> repeated reuse -> compounding downstream effects -> large late-stage damage
why_it_destroys_or_damages_trust: High-stakes systems cannot afford to dismiss 'small' flaws that will be replayed thousands of times.
direct_damage_to_study: The owner keeps internalizing and teaching something that looked too minor to investigate early.
long_horizon_damage: The library becomes historically contaminated by neglected small defects.
